rule: Stable generated behavior must be changed through operation/primitive IR, contracts, and input-output cases first. Generated implementations and wrapper adapters are projections or boundary smoke unless this manifest names an explicit hand-owned runtime or test boundary.

layers.title: Portable primitive contract
layers.owned_by: AW contracts for product primitives; command-generation for reusable primitive execution machinery
layers.direct_edit_policy: Edit primitive contracts or accepted package-domain runtime primitive implementations only; do not encode ordinary generated command behavior in hand-owned code.
layers.proof_route: uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-completion-blockers --format json
layers.allowed_hand_owned_code: Only live integration, mutation policy, provider/runtime boundary, package-specific judgment, or temporary extraction gaps with a retirement path.
layers.allowed_handwritten_tests: Primitive-boundary and package-domain runtime tests that prove behavior not expressible as portable IR yet.
layers.conversion_or_retirement_condition: Move to command-generation primitives or operation IR when behavior is deterministic, portable, and not package-domain policy.

layers.title: Operation IR and operation contracts
layers.owned_by: AW and module operation contract owners
layers.direct_edit_policy: Ordinary behavior changes should update operation contracts, primitive references, schemas, or conformance cases before generated artifacts.
layers.proof_route: uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/run_operation_conformance_tests.py --target python
layers.allowed_hand_owned_code: Runtime primitive implementation may exist only behind named primitive refs and accepted retained boundaries.
layers.allowed_handwritten_tests: Operation-contract checks may stay when they validate source-of-truth integrity rather than duplicate generated behavior.
layers.conversion_or_retirement_condition: A handwritten behavior regression should become an IR-backed input-output case when the behavior is stable and adapter-independent.

layers.title: Generated implementation artifacts
layers.direct_edit_policy: Do not hand-edit generated implementation artifacts for product behavior; update source contracts, IR, cases, or command-generation renderers.
layers.proof_route: uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py
layers.allowed_hand_owned_code: None for generated behavior; only explicitly accepted runtime primitive call boundaries may remain outside generated artifacts.
layers.allowed_handwritten_tests: Freshness, packaging, importability, and adapter conformance tests that prove generated output matches source contracts.
layers.conversion_or_retirement_condition: Direct behavior assertions over generated code should move to conformance cases unless they prove packaging/freshness/import mechanics.

layers.title: Target extension contract
layers.owned_by: command-generation for reusable target mechanics; AW for target support consumption and product-specific declarations
layers.direct_edit_policy: Do not add target-specific per-operation product semantics in AW; express target projection, dependencies, packaging, and conformance execution through reusable machinery.
layers.proof_route: uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py validates AW target support through command-generation target-extension contracts and matrix projection.
layers.allowed_hand_owned_code: AW may keep only product runtime primitives or adapter-boundary declarations, not reusable target execution logic.
layers.allowed_handwritten_tests: Consumer integration tests that prove AW consumes target support declarations and rejects per-operation target maintenance.
layers.conversion_or_retirement_condition: Retire any AW-side scaffolding once all implemented targets are declared through target_support.json and reusable command-generation matrix checks.

layers.title: Wrapper and transport adapter
layers.direct_edit_policy: Wrapper code/tests may own argv, help, errors, stdout, stderr, exit codes, and transport behavior; they must not be the default semantic proof when direct operation artifacts exist.
layers.proof_route: uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success plus focused wrapper tests for changed transports.
layers.allowed_hand_owned_code: Transport/wrapper glue and runtime boundary adapters only.
layers.allowed_handwritten_tests: CLI/MCP/transport smoke tests that prove boundary semantics, not underlying operation behavior.
layers.conversion_or_retirement_condition: Demote semantic wrapper assertions after direct operation conformance covers the behavior.

layers.title: IR-backed conformance case
layers.owned_by: AW conformance case owners with generic execution supplied by command-generation
layers.direct_edit_policy: Represent stable behavior as input, fixtures/effects, expected result or structured error, operation ref, and adapter target.
layers.proof_route: uv run python scripts/check/run_operation_conformance_tests.py --target python; target-specific conformance when target support exists.
layers.allowed_hand_owned_code: Only runner orchestration or AW-specific fixtures not yet expressible by reusable command-generation case machinery.
layers.allowed_handwritten_tests: Minimal higher-order tests for case selection, proof routing, and runner integration.
layers.conversion_or_retirement_condition: Replace one-off regression tests with minimal conceptually named cases when primitive and operation semantics are available.

layers.title: Retained ordinary handwritten test boundary
layers.owned_by: AW test owners named by retained boundary records
layers.direct_edit_policy: Keep ordinary tests only for runner internals, wrapper behavior, live integration, mutation policy, provider/runtime boundaries, high-risk workflow trust semantics, or narrow not-yet-expressible bug repros.
layers.proof_route: Focused pytest or package checks named by the retained boundary owner.
layers.allowed_hand_owned_code: Not applicable; ordinary tests can only justify retained proof, not product implementation ownership.
layers.allowed_handwritten_tests: Allowed only when the retained boundary record names owner, proof route, keep reason, and conversion or retirement condition.
layers.conversion_or_retirement_condition: Move stable behavior into operation conformance cases when equivalent black-box behavior can be expressed in simplified IR.

retained_hand_owned_boundaries.keep_reason: Exact source-symbol boundaries cover live integration, mutation policy, provider/runtime boundaries, package-specific judgment, and temporary extraction gaps.
retained_hand_owned_boundaries.conversion_or_retirement_condition: Retire or move to command-generation/IR when the symbol becomes deterministic portable behavior or when a smaller primitive can preserve the package policy.
retained_hand_owned_boundaries.stale_when: A retained symbol lacks source-symbol proof, owner, primitive refs, conformance ref, minimization route, or stale condition.

retained_hand_owned_boundaries.keep_reason: Root runtime behavior still includes live workspace inspection, recovery, and policy judgment that should not be hidden as generated command behavior.
retained_hand_owned_boundaries.conversion_or_retirement_condition: Move extractable deterministic behavior to contracts, command-generation, or generated IR when the audit marks it stable.
retained_hand_owned_boundaries.stale_when: The audit uses a closed issue as live ownership, omits extraction/guard candidates, or loses direct CLI edit routing.

retained_hand_owned_boundaries.proof_route: uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success plus focused wrapper pytest for changed commands
retained_hand_owned_boundaries.keep_reason: Wrappers own argv, help, error, stdout, stderr, exit-code, and transport semantics that direct operation conformance intentionally does not prove.
retained_hand_owned_boundaries.conversion_or_retirement_condition: Remove semantic assertions from wrapper tests after direct operation conformance covers the underlying behavior.
retained_hand_owned_boundaries.stale_when: A wrapper test is used as the default semantic proof for operation behavior while a direct operation artifact exists.

retained_hand_owned_boundaries.proof_route: Focused pytest for changed proof, packaging, lifecycle, start/implement/report/defaults, and dependency-integration surfaces
retained_hand_owned_boundaries.keep_reason: These tests prove AW installed-product routing, proof selection, generated freshness, dependency consumption, and workflow trust semantics rather than generic command-generation behavior.
retained_hand_owned_boundaries.conversion_or_retirement_condition: Move stable operation behavior to conformance cases and move generic generation behavior to command-generation tests.
retained_hand_owned_boundaries.stale_when: A retained AW test duplicates generic command-generation machinery or a stable operation behavior case without a boundary rationale.

retained_ordinary_test_groups.owner: AW proof routing and migration gates
retained_ordinary_test_groups.keep_reason: Proves AW proof selector behavior, completion gates, Docker posture, crash classification, retry semantics, and runtime-source boundary checks rather than generic generated-command behavior.
retained_ordinary_test_groups.future_conversion_condition: Move any deterministic adapter execution or primitive behavior to command-generation or operation conformance when the test no longer depends on AW proof-routing posture.
retained_ordinary_test_groups.durable_boundary_rationale: AW owns when generated package proof is adequate for workspace completion; command-generation owns only reusable execution primitives.

retained_ordinary_test_groups.keep_reason: Validates AW manifests, operation contracts, artifact registry routing, generated mirror freshness, and live CLI compatibility as source-of-truth integrity checks.
retained_ordinary_test_groups.future_conversion_condition: Move generic renderer, primitive, or adapter mechanics to command-generation tests; move stable black-box operation behavior to operation conformance cases.
retained_ordinary_test_groups.durable_boundary_rationale: AW must retain package-specific contract integrity and generated-resource visibility tests even when behavior execution is IR-backed.

retained_ordinary_test_groups.keep_reason: Proves changed-path selection chooses generated-package and operation-conformance proof routes for AW workspace changes.
retained_ordinary_test_groups.future_conversion_condition: Collapse into generated proof-routing contract cases when route selection is fully represented as declarative source-path to proof-lane mappings; move any reusable route-evaluation machinery to command-generation.
retained_ordinary_test_groups.durable_boundary_rationale: AW owns proof selection for its installed workspace lifecycle; reusable conformance execution alone cannot prove the routing decision.

retained_ordinary_test_groups.keep_reason: Covers AW tool contracts, filesystem side effects, adapter write guards, and local workspace safety boundaries.
retained_ordinary_test_groups.future_conversion_condition: Convert stable command behavior to operation conformance and move generic process-runner assertions to command-generation when filesystem safety is not the subject.
retained_ordinary_test_groups.durable_boundary_rationale: The retained behavior is local workspace safety and write-guard policy, not generic command invocation.

retained_ordinary_test_groups.keep_reason: Proves AW pins and consumes command-generation fragments, target support, and host primitive handlers correctly.
retained_ordinary_test_groups.future_conversion_condition: Move behavior to command-generation when it proves generic package capability; keep only consumer pinning, projection, and host-manifest integration in AW.
retained_ordinary_test_groups.durable_boundary_rationale: AW must prove the specific dependency boundary it consumes even when the upstream package owns reusable implementation semantics.

retained_ordinary_test_groups.test_surface: Packaging, lifecycle, skills, start/implement/report/defaults, and module tests that mention generated surfaces
retained_ordinary_test_groups.proof_route: Focused pytest for the changed installed-product, lifecycle, startup, report, defaults, or module surface
retained_ordinary_test_groups.keep_reason: Asserts shipping, install/upgrade, ordinary routing, compact output, and generated-resource visibility in the AW product rather than reusable generator behavior.
retained_ordinary_test_groups.future_conversion_condition: Move stable operation input-output behavior to conformance cases and move reusable generated artifact checks to command-generation once the installed-product posture is no longer the subject.
retained_ordinary_test_groups.durable_boundary_rationale: Installed-product behavior is an AW domain boundary: users experience generated resources through AW startup, proof, lifecycle, and package surfaces.

closure_rule: #1476 can be resolved only after all layers have direct proof or owner-approved rejection evidence and #1484 records the closure inventory and bypass guardrails.
